steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence establishes a general claim: Belew had talent for “intricately and expertly detailed costume design.” The second sentence offers a concrete example of that talent — the blue rhinestones. The transition must signal that the second sentence is a specific illustration of the general claim already made, not a new time point or a contrasting idea.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Next,: Signals a step in a temporal or sequential series. The rhinestones are not the next event in a sequence — they are an example of the design talent already described. ✗
  • Choice B — In particular,: Signals that what follows is a specific instance of the general point just made. The rhinestones are precisely that: a specific detail illustrating Belew's careful attention to detail. ✓
  • Choice C — Soon,: Signals a short time later. There is no time gap being described; the second sentence elaborates on the same suit. ✗
  • Choice D — Nevertheless,: Signals contrast or concession. The rhinestones confirm Belew's skill; they do not contradict anything. ✗
B is correct.
